item.title: EMI Teaching Course Demonstration
item.description:
Steve Ying-Chu Chen is designed to facilitate a supportive EMI learning environment. You will receive insights into how I structure academic literacy within the field of Sport Sciences.

Clear Objectives, Scaffolded Learning, Active Participation!

On this page, you’ll find the EMI course demonstration of how I utilize digital resources to teach core concepts like Experimental Research Design and the distinction between Acute exercise and Cognitive Function.

Physical Activity and Cognitive Function

Visual Scaffolding for Complex Concepts

In my EMI course demonstration, Physical Activity and Cognitive Function, I utilize visual aids to explain abstract methodological concepts. [cite_start]As demonstrated in my teaching presentation, visualizing the relationship between the Independent Variable (Cause) and the Dependent Variable (Effect) is crucial for student understanding.

This video serves as a flipped-classroom resource to help students visualize how we manipulate variables—such as comparing High-Intensity Interval Training (HIIT) vs. Moderate Continuous Training—to measure outcomes like short-term memory.

Watch on YouTube

Youtube:

{{< youtube X1pHnvnacZA >}}

Clear Objectives, Scaffolded Learning, Active Participation!

On this page, you’ll find examples of how I utilize digital resources to teach core concepts like Statistical Analysis and Experimental Design.

Linear Regression Course Guided Reading EMI course Demonstration

Simplifying Abstract Concepts with Analogies

Teaching statistics in an EMI setting can be challenging due to the complex terminology. In this “Guided Reading” session, I demonstrate how to use a relatable Real-world Analogy—the “Lunch Scenario”—to explain the concept of Ordinary Least Squares (OLS) and its sensitivity to outliers.

By asking students to imagine 9 people buying a $10 lunch versus 1 person buying a $500 meal, I help them visualize how a single data point can distort the regression line. This Analogy-based Scaffolding is a key strategy I use to make abstract mathematical models accessible to students.

Youtube:

{{< youtube OFHVSfIVx-0 >}}

Did you find this page helpful? Consider sharing it 🙌

item.title: Knowledge monetization
item.description:
Ying Chu Che’s CV website is designed to give knowledge-based workers a seamless experience. You will receive numerous potential application insight of knowledge.

Your Knowledge, Your Platform, Your Future!

On this page, you’ll find some examples of the types of real application that can be rendered within your field.

YouTube Video

Teach your course or sell your idea by sharing videos with your potential clients. Providing valuable content will build trust, establish your authority, and attract the right audience who are genuinely interested in what you offer. Whether you’re sharing insights, tutorials, or behind-the-scenes processes, consistent and engaging videos can turn your knowledge into a powerful tool for connection and conversion.

Youtube:

{{< youtube a-jPzFZW5_c >}}

Podcast

You’ll gain insights into personal training and exercise science through my podcast, where evidence-based knowledge meets real-world application.

In each episode, I share expert perspectives on fitness, health, and human performance—combining scientific research with practical tips for trainers, athletes, and health-conscious individuals. Whether you’re looking to deepen your understanding of biomechanics, training strategies, or the latest findings in exercise and sport sciences, this podcast is your gateway to informed, actionable knowledge. 我是運科竹 channel:

👉 Click to listen the channel!
我是運科竹 Podcast

Vocus and Medium writing

Vocus and Medium are two dynamic publishing platforms that empower individuals to share their knowledge, perspectives, and expertise with a global audience. Whether you’re a thought leader, educator, researcher, or entrepreneur, these platforms offer intuitive tools to transform your insights into engaging articles, essays, and narratives.

Vocus is widely adopted in Mandarin-speaking communities and is known for its reader-centric features, monetization opportunities, and strong social engagement functions. 我是運科竹 Vocus

Medium, on the other hand, offers a sleek, minimalist interface and access to a vast international network of readers, professionals, and niche communities. It’s an ideal space for English-language writing, especially if you’re aiming to build authority and connect globally. 我是運科竹 Medium

Both platforms prioritize authenticity, storytelling, and value-driven content, making them excellent choices for knowledge monetization and personal branding.

Did you find this page helpful? Consider sharing it 🙌
